Package BusinessLayer

Class Switch

java.lang.Object
BusinessLayer.Component
BusinessLayer.Switch
public class Switch extends Component
Represents a digital switch component in a circuit. The switch acts as a source component with a single output pin. Its state can be ON (true / 1) or OFF (false / 0), and it can toggle between these states. The output pin always reflects the current state. The component can be drawn on a canvas with a graphical representation showing its base, lever, and 0/1 labels.

  • Constructor Details

    • Switch

      public Switch(int x, int y)
      Constructs a new Switch at the given (x, y) coordinates.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the switch
      y - the y-coordinate of the switch

  • Method Details

    • initializePins

      protected void initializePins()
      Initializes the output pin of the switch. A switch has only one output pin located on its right side.
      Specified by:
      initializePins in class Component

    • updatePinPositions

      protected void updatePinPositions()
      Updates the output pin position when the switch is moved.
      Specified by:
      updatePinPositions in class Component

    • compute

      public void compute()
      Updates the output pin value to match the switch state.
      Specified by:
      compute in class Component

    • toggle

      public void toggle()
      Toggles the switch state between ON and OFF. Also updates the output pin value to reflect the new state.

    • setState

      public void setState(boolean state)
      Sets the switch state explicitly.
      Parameters:
      state - true for ON, false for OFF

    • getState

      public boolean getState()
      Returns the current state of the switch.
      Returns:
      true if ON, false if OFF

    • cloneComponent

      public Component cloneComponent()
      Creates a clone of this switch, including its state and selection status.
      Specified by:
      cloneComponent in class Component
      Returns:
      a cloned Switch component

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics g)
      Draws the switch on a graphics canvas. Includes the base rectangle, lever, 0/1 labels, and input/output lines. Also draws a highlight if the switch is selected.
      Specified by:
      draw in class Component
      Parameters:
      g - the Graphics object used for drawing
